integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with author: operator, status: pending, and approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-202'
revision_summary: EXP-202 revised in place (PROP-009 additive edit) per two operator clarifications. Change 1: fonts are now self-hosted under docs/fonts/ as three variable WOFF2 files (Source Serif 4, Inter, JetBrains Mono) plus OFL LICENSES.md — no Google Fonts <link> tag, no preconnect, no third-party font origin. Change 2: introduces NC8 (revised) — 'No third-party runtime dependencies, with ONE grandfathered exception: GA4 property G-QVD21HWH5X (loaded via googletagmanager.com/gtag/js)'. Change 3: anticipated_objections #2 corrected — the pre-revision claim of 'zero third-party requests' was a factual error (GA has loaded gtag.js all along); the corrected framing cites the curmudgeon's c_4 finding and CLARIFICATION-002 explicitly. Change 4: acceptance_criteria updated to test NC8 compliance (no new third-party hosts beyond existing GA endpoints; tcpdump/HAR confirmation; same-origin WOFF2 download count == 3). Change 5: new curmudgeon_finding_responses field records the c_4 resolution and requests the decider close c_4 as addressed on integration.

files_to_classify_as_git_owned: docs/fonts/source-serif-4-variable.woff2 (or per-weight statics if variable is infeasible)
required_updates: Add entries to the OWNERSHIP dictionary in build.js (or add glob 'docs/fonts/**' => 'git' if the mechanism supports it).
Add corresponding entries to OWNED_BY_GIT in monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md.
Per CLAUDE.md 'File Ownership Rules', the two lists MUST be kept in sync.
file_ownership_flag_for_decider.note_to_decider: This EXP is the first to introduce a binary-asset directory under docs/. If OWNERSHIP does not support prefix/glob entries cleanly, the decider may need to either enumerate the four files individually or extend the OWNERSHIP mechanism. Flagging so this is not a silent integration landmine.

curmudgeon_findings_addressed_by_this_re.id: c_4 (per operator's numbering; substantively the 'critical' privacy-premise-error hole in curmudgeon review holes_found)
curmudgeon_findings_addressed_by_this_re.requested_decider_action: close c_4 as addressed on integration of the revised EXP-202

curmudgeon_findings_outstanding: c_2 (major): --rule contrast 1.30:1 dark / 1.35:1 light — fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.11 3:1 for non-text UI components. Not addressed by this revision (operator clarifications did not scope it). Candidate fixes: raise --rule dark to ~#4a4a6a and --rule light to ~#c8c0a8 — OR defer --rule from A1 to first-consumer EXP.
c_3 (major): --semantic-accent dark #7eb8da is byte-identical to existing --heading dark #7eb8da — undocumented hex collision. Add a one-sentence note or pick a distinct hex (e.g. #6aa8cd).

c_5 (moderate): color-blind risk on --semantic-warn vs --semantic-crit — both red/orange family, modest luminance separation. Either widen the luminance gap at token definition time, or sequence EXP-A8 glyph+label pairing before first consumer of these tokens.

minor findings on --ink-3 speculative, monospace literal count 3 vs 6, Bunny Fonts framing, build-reproducibility scope, and the CC4 warm-grey-on-cool-bg claim — all listed in notes_for_human_review_group.OUTSTANDING CURMUDGEON FINDINGS bullet of the revised EXP for the operator group review packet.
hard_hold_reaffirmed: This revision does NOT unlock integration. status=DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW, expansion_type=FOUNDATIONAL_MODEL_CHANGE, and integration_blocked_until are unchanged per CLARIFICATION-002's explicit DO-NOT list. The decider must continue to leave this EXP in the tracker as revised+held until the named approval HNOTE arrives.
